ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the tweet was posted */,
  "user_id" TEXT /* Reference to the user who posted the tweet */,
  "tweet_content" TEXT /* Content of the tweet */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL to the tweet */,
  "has_media" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet contains media */,
  "is_reply" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet is a reply */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "medias" TEXT /* List of media associated with the tweet */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographic location of the tweet */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet */,
  "in_reply_to_user_id" TEXT /* User ID of the user being replied to */,
  "in_reply_to_tweet_id" TEXT /* Tweet ID of the tweet being replied to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the Twitter account */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "friends_count" INTEGER /* Number of friends the user follows */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile image */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* User's bio or description */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographic location of the user */,
  "protected" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user's account is protected */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user's account is verified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis record */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* Reference to the tweet being analyzed */,
  "neg" REAL /* Negative sentiment score */,
  "neu" REAL /* Neutral sentiment score */,
  "pos" REAL /* Positive sentiment score */,
  "compound" REAL /* Compound sentiment score representing overall sentiment */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the sentiment analysis */,
  "sentiment_label" TEXT /* Label for the sentiment */,
  "analysis_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the sentiment analysis was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_analysis_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement metric record */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* Reference to the tweet */,
  "nbr_favorite" INTEGER /* Number of favorites (likes) for the tweet */,
  "nbr_reply" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the tweet */,
  "nbr_retweet" INTEGER /* Number of retweets for the tweet */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been quoted */,
  "engagement_score" REAL /* Overall engagement score calculated from likes, replies, retweets, and quotes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_metrics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "media" (
  "media_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each media item */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* Reference to the tweet associated with the media */,
  "media_url" TEXT /* URL of the media resource */,
  "media_type" TEXT /* Type of media (e.g., image, video) */,
  "media_size" INTEGER /* Size of the media file */,
  "media_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the media file (for videos) */,
  "media_format" TEXT /* Format of the media file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("media_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_media_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag record */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* Reference to the tweet */,
  "hashtag" TEXT /* Hashtag text */,
  "hashtag_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the hashtag appears in the tweet */,
  "hashtag_frequency" REAL /* Frequency of the hashtag in the overall dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hashtags_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "replies" (
  "reply_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reply record */,
  "original_tweet_id" TEXT /* Reference to the original tweet */,
  "reply_tweet_id" TEXT /* Reference to the reply tweet */,
  "reply_content" TEXT /* Content of the reply tweet */,
  "reply_datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the reply was posted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reply_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_original_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("original_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_reply_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("reply_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "retweets" (
  "retweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each retweet record */,
  "original_tweet_id" TEXT /* Reference to the original tweet */,
  "retweet_tweet_id" TEXT /* Reference to the retweet tweet */,
  "retweet_content" TEXT /* Content of the retweet */,
  "retweet_datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the retweet was posted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("retweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retweets_retweet_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("retweet_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retweets_original_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("original_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend record */,
  "topic" TEXT /* Trending topic */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp of the trend occurrence */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Overall sentiment score associated with the topic */,
  "trend_volume" INTEGER /* Number of tweets associated with the trend */,
  "trend_source" TEXT /* Source of the trend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trends_topic FOREIGN KEY ("topic") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_content")
)

CREATE TABLE "users_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the role record */,
  "user_id" TEXT /* Reference to the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role assigned to the user (e.g., analyst, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object or array of permissions associated with the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
